title: Thattekad Bird Sanctuary
focus: Ecological features, location, and recent faunal survey of Thattekad Bird Sanctuary.
rationale: A distinct protected area with specific geographical and ecological facts, such as being Kerala's first bird sanctuary and its association with the Periyar and Idamalayar rivers.
sectionExcerpt: Kerala Thattekad Bird Sanctuary - Context: Faunal survey adds nine new species in Thattekad Bird Sanctuary. Location: Ernakulam District, Kerala. Kerala's first bird sanctuary, recognized as one of richest ecological zones in Western Ghats. Forest type: Tropical Evergreen Forests, Tropical Semi-evergreen forests, Moist Deciduous forests, Riparian Forests... River: Periyar and Idamalayar rivers.
evidenceBullets: Located in Ernakulam District, Kerala.
Recognized as Kerala's first bird sanctuary.
Situated along the Periyar and Idamalayar rivers.
Forest types include Tropical Evergreen, Semi-evergreen, Moist Deciduous, and Riparian.

title: Sahyadri Tiger Reserve
focus: Translocation of tigers, location, and ecological profile of Sahyadri Tiger Reserve.
rationale: An important tiger reserve in the Western Ghats with specific reservoirs and a recent translocation context, making it highly examinable for environment and geography.
sectionExcerpt: Maharashtra Sahyadri Tiger Reserve - Context: MoEFCC granted approval for Translocation of 8 Tigers from Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve and Pench Tiger Reserve to Sahyadri Tiger Reserve... Northern most tiger habitat in Western Ghats. Notified in 2010 by amalgamating the Chandoli National Park and Koyna Wildlife Sanctuary. Reservoirs: 'Shivsagar' reservoir of Koyana River and 'Vasant Sagar' reservoir of Warana River.
evidenceBullets: Northernmost tiger habitat in the Western Ghats.
Formed in 2010 by amalgamating Chandoli National Park and Koyna Wildlife Sanctuary.
Contains the 'Shivsagar' reservoir of the Koyana River and the 'Vasant Sagar' reservoir of the Warana River.
Received approval for tiger translocation from Tadoba-Andhari and Pench Tiger Reserves.

title: Keibul Lamjao National Park
focus: Unique floating park ecosystem, Loktak Lake, and Sangai deer habitat.
rationale: Highly examinable due to its unique status as the world's only floating national park, its association with Loktak Lake (Ramsar/Montreux), and as the habitat for the Sangai deer.
sectionExcerpt: Manipur Keibul Lamjao National Park - Context: Study links land use to water pollution in Manipur's Loktak Lake. Location: Bishnupur and Thoubal district of Manipur. Only floating park in the world. Largest area of Phumdi (floating Islands of vegetation) in Loktak lake is in this park. Loktak lake, a Ramsar site and Montreux Record site... Fauna: Brow-antlered deer (Sangai).
evidenceBullets: The only floating national park in the world.
Located in Loktak Lake, which is both a Ramsar site and a Montreux Record site.
Characterized by Phumdis, which are floating islands of vegetation.
Primary habitat for the Brow-antlered deer, also known as Sangai.

title: Similipal Tiger Reserve
focus: Notification as a National Park, geographical features, and tribal populations of Similipal.
rationale: Rich in geographical facts including rivers, waterfalls, and tribal demographics, making it a prime candidate for UPSC geography and environment questions.
sectionExcerpt: Odisha Similipal Tiger Reserve - Context: Odisha notified Similipal Tiger Reserve as National Park making it largest in Odisha, leaving behind Bhitarkanika. Location: Mayurbhanj District, Odisha in Chottanagpur region. Rivers: Burhabalanga, Palpala Bandan, Salandi, Kahairi, and Deo... Waterfalls: Joranda and Barehipani. Tribes Found: Kolha, Santhala, Bhumija... Note: Similipal is a Biosphere reserve under the UNESCO Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme.
evidenceBullets: Now the largest National Park in Odisha, surpassing Bhitarkanika.
Drained by rivers such as Burhabalanga, Salandi, and Deo.
Features the Joranda and Barehipani waterfalls.
Inhabited by tribes including Kolha, Santhala, Mankadia, and Sahara.
Recognized as a Biosphere Reserve under the UNESCO MAB Programme.

title: Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve
focus: Composition, location, and associated protected areas of Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve.
rationale: Spans two states and includes multiple wildlife sanctuaries and tiger reserves, which is a common format for UPSC matching questions.
sectionExcerpt: Tamil Nadu Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve - Context: SC Orders Probe into Violations of Forest and Wildlife Laws in Tamil Nadu's Agasthyamalai Landscape. Location: Located in Southern Western Ghats in Tamil Nadu and Kerala. Includes 3 WLSs: Shendurney, Peppara and Neyyar (Kerala), and Kalakad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve (Tamil Nadu)... Rivers: Thamirabarai River.
evidenceBullets: Located in the Southern Western Ghats across Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
Includes Shendurney, Peppara, and Neyyar Wildlife Sanctuaries in Kerala.
Includes Kalakad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve in Tamil Nadu.
Associated with the Thamirabarai River.

title: Dudhwa Tiger Reserve
focus: Location, habitat, and recent leopard population growth in Dudhwa Tiger Reserve.
rationale: An important protected area in the Terai region with specific river associations and habitat types.
sectionExcerpt: Uttar Pradesh Dudhwa Tiger Reserve (DTR) - Context: Leopard population in DTR has grown by 198.91% since 2022. Location: Terai region of Uttar Pradesh, along Indo-Nepal Border. Includes Dudhwa National Park and Katerniaghat and Kishanpur. Forest type: Typical Tarai-Bhabar habitat of upper Gangetic plains... Rivers: Mohana, Suheli, Joraha, Nagro etc.
evidenceBullets: Located in the Terai region of Uttar Pradesh along the Indo-Nepal Border.
Comprises Dudhwa National Park, Katerniaghat, and Kishanpur.
Characterized by typical Tarai-Bhabar habitat of the upper Gangetic plains.
Drained by rivers such as Mohana, Suheli, Joraha, and Nagro.

title: Salkhan Fossil Park
focus: Geo-heritage significance, stromatolites, and Great Oxidation Event connection of Salkhan Fossil Park.
rationale: A unique geological site added to the UNESCO Tentative List, linking biology (cyanobacteria) with Earth's history (Great Oxidation Event).
sectionExcerpt: Salkhan Fossil Park - Context: Salkhan Fossil Park added to UNESCO Tentative List For World Heritage Sites, officially known as Sonbhadra Fossils Park, Sonbhadra, Uttar Pradesh... Location: Kaimur Range (part of Vindhya range), adjacent to Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary. One of oldest and most well-preserved sites in world (fossils dating ~1.4 billion years). Site's fossil assemblage features Stromatolites created by communities of cyanobacteria... record Great Oxidation Event.
evidenceBullets: Added to the UNESCO Tentative List for World Heritage Sites.
Located in the Kaimur Range, part of the Vindhya range in Uttar Pradesh.
Features approximately 1.4 billion-year-old Stromatolite fossils created by cyanobacteria.
The fossilized microbial structures record the Great Oxidation Event.

title: Bhagirathi Eco Sensitive Zone
focus: Ecological significance, regulatory framework, and location of the Bhagirathi ESZ.
rationale: Covers the concept of Eco-Sensitive Zones (ESZ) and specific environmental governance (Zonal Master Plan) in a fragile Himalayan stretch.
sectionExcerpt: Uttarakhand Bhagirathi Eco Sensitive Zone (ESZ) - Context: Recently, Uttarakhand Government granted approval for Netala bypass project in fragile Bhagirathi ESZ despite previous rejections... Created to protect River Ganga's most pristine stretch between Gaumukh and Uttarkashi town. Eco-sensitive Zones are ecologically fragile areas around Protected Areas... Notification: By the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC) in 2012... under Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
evidenceBullets: Created to protect the pristine stretch of the River Ganga between Gaumukh and Uttarkashi.
Notified by MoEF&CC under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
Eco-sensitive Zones act as 'Shock Absorbers' around Protected Areas and wildlife corridors.
Requires a Zonal Master Plan (ZMP) based on a watershed approach.

title: Sundarbans Tiger Reserve
focus: Expansion, geographical borders, and international designations of Sundarbans Tiger Reserve.
rationale: India's second-largest tiger reserve with a unique mangrove habitat and multiple international tags (UNESCO, CA|TS, MAB).
sectionExcerpt: West Bengal. Sundarbans Tiger Reserve (STR). Context: STR is now India's second largest after expansion of its area was approved by National Board of Wildlife (NBWL). - Now STR is just behind Andhra Pradesh's Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve in size... Only mangrove forest (with Bangladesh) housing a significant tiger population. Borders: Bangladesh (east), Bay of Bengal (south), River Matla (west), Rivers Bidya & Gomdi (north-west). Associated Designations: UNESCO World Heritage Site, CA|TS (Conservation Assured | Tiger Standards), MAB.
evidenceBullets: Now India's second-largest tiger reserve, behind Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam.
The only mangrove forest in the world housing a significant tiger population.
Bordered by the River Matla to the west and Rivers Bidya & Gomdi to the north-west.
Holds designations as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, CA|TS, and MAB Programme site.
